sub_pixel_system.notes: No sub-pixel system documentation available for NES pinball game

special_mechanics.name: Pinball Physics
special_mechanics.description: Game uses pinball ball physics rather than traditional character movement. Player controls a silver pinball on various themed tables using flippers to keep the ball in play and accomplish objectives.

physics_values.noted_as_poor_simulation: Contemporary reviews note the game is not an accurate pinball simulation

special_mechanics.name: Flipper Mechanics
special_mechanics.description: Players use flippers to control the silver pinball and keep it in play. Upgraded flippers can be purchased at the Black Market using earned gold currency.

special_mechanics.name: Stopper Mechanics
special_mechanics.description: Stoppers are gameplay elements that can be upgraded via the Black Market. Graphics show stoppers marked with '1 F P' (used version) and '1 2 3' (unused version), rendered white in-game.

tier_notes: Tier 3 (empirical/qualitative). Pinball Quest is a genre-hybrid pinball-RPG game rather than a traditional character-movement platformer. No precise physics parameters are documented in available sources. Game uses pinball ball physics governed by flipper mechanics rather than player character movement. Contemporary and modern reviews criticize it as a poor pinball simulation.
sources_used: The Cutting Room Floor - Pinball Quest article
